scope: Affirmative hard-negative proposals for every E14 mechanism plus an immutable queue for genuinely independent review. No dossier is self-accepted.
digestPolicy: Generated dossier contentSha256 is SHA-256 of UTF-8 locator, newline and curated summary. It binds the local evidence assertion, not a remote byte snapshot.

evidenceAssertions.summary: The BIS reports that core fixed-income and most other markets smoothly absorbed the brief Brexit turbulence with little or no disruption to functioning.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: Federal Reserve staff report that corporate-bond bid-ask spreads widened but not unusually, substantial margin calls were met, and trading and liquidity remained generally orderly.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The minutes record volatile asset prices, brief foreign-exchange liquidity strains, falling global equities and wider credit spreads immediately after the vote.
The FDIC annual report states that insured banks posted solid or record earnings and strengthened balance sheets by increasing capital and reducing troubled assets in 1994.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: FOMC minutes describe robust domestic credit demand and monetary growth while the Mexican peso crisis was transmitting through international financial markets.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The same minutes document sharp peso depreciation, spillovers to other developing economies and pressure on the dollar, so containment was not universal across mechanisms.

hardNegativeBlueprints.boundaryRationale: June contains the referendum shock and July contains the documented rapid normalization, allowing the dossier to test broad market resilience without extending into later negotiations.

The two-month window captures the immediate liquidity stress test and subsequent normalization documented by two authorities, while retaining the observed brief foreign-exchange strains as counterevidence.

The boundary isolates a major foreign political and financial shock whose immediate global transmission was explicitly assessed as contained and orderly, without claiming absence of longer-run economic effects.

The interval begins with the peso break and ends after the acute spillover window, while contemporaneous U.S. banking evidence documents strong lending, capital and credit quality rather than inferred calm.

limitations: All four dossiers are proposed hard negatives, not accepted labels.
The same Brexit event supplies mechanism-specific contrasts; future evaluation must keep each mechanism separate and must not count the event as three independent shocks.
Evidence digests bind curated assertions and locators, not archived remote bytes.
No independent review receipt exists at freeze time; self-acceptance is forbidden.
